title: tordylium aegyptiacum var. palaestinum
intro: Tordylium aegyptiacum var. palaestinum, also known as the Palestinian wild celery or Torilis, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Tordylium aegyptiacum var. palaestinum include its potential to help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
